summary: Two "Yolka" air defense drones conducted a simultaneous kinetic intercept of an enemy fixed-wing UAV in a Russian region, with two mobile fire team crews delivering a double strike. On April 2, 2026, operators from the 1st Separate Special Operations Center, in coordination with Ukrainian Defense Intelligence, struck military targets in occupied Crimea, destroying an enemy UAV storage facility, an aircraft, and a radar station, including a site storing "Orion" strike and reconnaissance drones valued at over $5 million each. Russian forces dropped fake money with QR codes from "Gerbera" drones over border areas as a psychological operation. In the Zaporizhzhia region, an airstrike on a Ukrainian air defense system in Vozdvizhevka was captured by an FPV drone. Ukrainian soldiers from the 36th Marine Brigade survived a Russian FPV drone attack near Kostiantynivka, with one soldier holding a drone detector at 0:11 in the footage. FPV footage showed a Russian Ka-52 being downed near Nadiivka, Donetsk region, with additional drones striking the crew during their evacuation attempt.
